mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero when rates rise.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses the 4.5% threshold, it signals a deteriorating labor market that reduces consumer spending capacity and corporate profit growth, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in >55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in real personal consumption expenditures and industrial production indicates synchronized demand and supply expansion, which boosts corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce positive forward returns when either series declines, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of monetary tightening, a sustained rise in industrial production reflects resilient economic activity that offsets higher borrowing costs, generating positive short-term S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The signal reverses direction during tightening cycles, producing negative 5-day forward returns in >60% of cases, or loses significance when FEDFUNDS increases by >0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls directly increases household income and consumer demand, which translates into higher corporate earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative or flat when PAYEMS increases, or the Rank IC falls below 0.01 across multiple rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above the 0.4% monthly threshold forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, raising real discount rates and compressing equity multiples, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally following CPI spikes above 0.4%, or the predictive accuracy of the threshold drops below 45% in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in housing starts and real GDP growth signals broad-based economic strength and increased capital expenditure, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative forward returns when HOUST and GDPC1 both rise, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in panel regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Strong quarterly growth in real personal consumption expenditures above 2.0% indicates robust consumer demand that immediately boosts equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are negative in >50% of instances when RSAFS growth exceeds 2.0%, or the signal predictive power vanishes during recessionary periods.
